flags.concern: The plan is built around a three-tier allocator, but the repository's current test contract already expects a different coexistence algorithm. Step 4 says the existing shrink-to-fit test should still pass, yet the checked-in test file expects `300/300` for the default 600px case, `200/400` when generations is 400px, and `156/94` in a 250px viewport. Current `getEffectivePaneHeights()` only implements the visibility short-circuit, ideal-fit, and fixed 70:30 split, so removing the visibility gate without first resolving whether the new tests are authoritative will likely codify the wrong behavior.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to address both flags: (1) Added Phase 1 to implement the new gens-preserving allocation algorithm that the TDD-style tests expect, before removing the visibility gate. The old plan assumed the 70:30 split was correct and would be kept. (2) Enumerated ALL 6 test assertions affected by removing the visibility gate, computing exact expected values for each. The initial-state test (effectiveEditorHeight: 540→300, effectiveGensHeight: 300→300) and solo-editor test are now explicitly called out. Added a new stability test that proves heights don't change across visibility toggles.

flags.concern: Step 4 understates which existing assertions change if heights are always precomputed. The provider exposes `effective*` heights even when both panes are hidden, so the initial-state test will also fail after this change; the plan only updates the solo-editor test and says the rest are unchanged. Following the plan as written would leave `PanesContext.test.tsx` inconsistent with the new behavior.
flags.evidence: Revised the plan to address both flags: (1) Added Phase 1 to implement the new gens-preserving allocation algorithm that the TDD-style tests expect, before removing the visibility gate. The old plan assumed the 70:30 split was correct and would be kept. (2) Enumerated ALL 6 test assertions affected by removing the visibility gate, computing exact expected values for each. The initial-state test (effectiveEditorHeight: 540→300, effectiveGensHeight: 300→300) and solo-editor test are now explicitly called out. Added a new stability test that proves heights don't change across visibility toggles.

flags.concern: Scope creep: the plan now expands the original snap fix into designing a brand-new degraded proportional allocator, but that tier is still not actually specified. Step 2 says to "reverse-engineer" a formula from a single expected case (`250 -> 156/94`) and to try candidate formulas until one matches. That leaves the core behavior undefined and risks an ad hoc implementation that only satisfies one fixture rather than a defensible layout rule.
flags.evidence: The user idea only calls for pre-computing coexistence height on visibility toggle. In the repository, `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:87-126` has no degraded-tier logic today, and the only concrete degraded expectation is one test case at `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx:264-289`.

flags.concern: The validator warning is still unresolved: the plan does not fully meet the required step structure because not every step section includes a repository file reference in backticks. That means the plan still fails the stated formatting contract.
flags.evidence: The provided validator output still reports `Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.` `### Step 7: Manual verification` has no file reference, so the warning remains applicable.
